history.content:
<thought>
I see the agent at `>` on the top row inside the corridor. The goal `G` is to the right and slightly below; from my current position, moving forward will get me closer without running into lava (`L`) or walls. I will move forward one step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is now directly above empty space and still facing right. Ahead to the right of the current position is a floor cell, and there are no walls or lava in that immediate forward path. I will move forward one more step to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent has advanced to the right; I am facing right and the next cell ahead is the wall boundary (outside the corridor). To continue toward the goal below, I should turn right now would worsen alignment, so I will turn left to face downward/then navigate toward the lower corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up (`^`) but the goal is located below in the row containing `G`. The cell above is a wall boundary, so moving forward would fail. I should turn right to face east/west as needed; turning right from up will face right, which still keeps me from the goal row. Turning left from up will face left; however, the open corridor is to the right now blocked, so I will turn right twice? Since only one action allowed, I’ll turn right first to face right and reassess the forward cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing right (`>`). Ahead is the wall `#` at the boundary, so I cannot move forward. I need to head downward toward the goal at the bottom row. Turning left from right will face up, which is also blocked; turning right from right would face down (since right -> down with a right turn). I will turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I’m facing down (`v`). The cell in front is `.` (open) so I can move toward the goal row.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I moved forward and I’m at the cell above the lava, facing down. Ahead is `L`, which is not safe to enter. I should turn right to face left? From down (`v`), turning right faces left; turning left faces right. I need to avoid lava and go toward the goal at the bottom; the goal is left of my current column, so I should turn right to move left away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m now facing left (`<`) and directly to the left is `.` (safe). Moving forward will take me away from the lava while heading toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
